summaries: Between 11:14 and 12:53 UTC on 2024-04-08, {service} users routed to {pod} experienced intermittent authentication failures. Tokens with a valid TTL were being rejected as expired. Investigation confirmed that {node} had drifted {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of NTP consensus; the JWT library enforces a hard {tolerance} skew limit with no grace period. Pods on all other nodes authenticated successfully throughout the incident. NTP was restored at 12:53 UTC and error rate dropped to zero within 90 seconds.

On 2023-09-21, etcd leader election instability was observed across the production cluster. Raft heartbeats from {node} were being rejected by followers because the embedded timestamps fell outside the ±{tolerance} drift window. Root cause was identified as {node} clock running {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of cluster consensus following loss of NTP contact with {ntp_server} approximately {jitter} minutes prior. The cluster stabilized after a manual chrony step correction.

Tracking ID: SKW-20240119-003. Starting at 07:45 PST, approximately {error_pct}% of {service} API requests failed with HTTP 401. Analysis of access logs showed the failures were entirely confined to {pod}, while sibling pods on other nodes returned HTTP 200 for identical requests. The per-pod failure pattern indicated a node-local issue. Chrony on {node} reported an offset of {clock_offset} {clock_direction} with last NTP sync {jitter} minutes prior to alert.

The authentication issue affecting {service} has been resolved as of Tuesday, 2023-11-14 15:22 US/Pacific. For approximately 38 minutes, {pod} on {node} was rejecting JWT tokens with expiry {token_exp} because the local clock read {current_time} — a skew of {clock_offset} beyond the configured {tolerance} tolerance. All other pods validated the same tokens successfully. A chrony step correction resolved the offset and restored normal authentication.

09/07/2023 RCA — {service} — Distributed Auth. Summary of Impact: From 22:10 to 23:41 UTC, token validation errors affected {error_pct}% of requests to {service}. The failures were limited to a single pod ({pod}) running on {node}. Chrony reported {node} clock was {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of {ntp_server}. The NTP daemon had been OOM-killed and not automatically restarted, allowing drift to accumulate silently. Daemon was restarted at 23:41 UTC.

2024-07-03 Incident Summary — {service}. Starting at 04:22 UTC, distributed rate-limit enforcement became inconsistent. Clients routed to {node} were permitted to exceed configured thresholds while other nodes throttled the same clients correctly. Forensic review showed {node} clock was {clock_offset} {clock_direction}, causing rate-limit window resets to occur at the wrong intervals. Chrony offset corrected at 05:14 UTC; consistent enforcement resumed within one polling cycle.

Intermittent etcd lease expiry failures were observed on 2023-06-15 beginning at 17:30 UTC. Leases granted with a {token_exp}-second TTL appeared to expire before holders could renew them. The root cause was {node} running {clock_offset} {clock_direction}, which caused lease expiry timestamps — computed from local time — to be reached earlier than expected by the rest of the cluster. Manual clock correction at 18:05 UTC resolved the lease failures.

Post-Incident Report 2024-02-28 — {service}. A liveness probe masked the impact of an NTP failure on {node}. The pod ({pod}) responded 200 to all health checks while simultaneously rejecting {error_pct}% of business traffic with JWT validation errors. The health check does not exercise the token validation path. {node} had accumulated {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of drift; the JWT library's {tolerance} tolerance was exceeded. Chrony resync resolved the issue at 09:17 UTC.

Tracking ID: NTP-20230802-011. {service} observed a split-brain symptom in its authentication tier: roughly half of API requests succeeded while the other half returned 401, with the outcome determined entirely by which pod handled the request. Debugging confirmed {node} clock was {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of consensus. Tokens issued with expiry {token_exp} were valid on all other nodes but appeared expired on {pod}. NTP sync restored normal behavior at 14:44 UTC.

On 2024-10-17, {service} cache tier on {node} began generating elevated origin load due to premature cache entry eviction. Objects set with a standard TTL were expiring {clock_offset} early because {node} clock was running {clock_direction}. This increased origin fetch rate by approximately 4x for traffic routed to {node}. No data loss occurred. NTP resync corrected the drift and cache hit rates normalized over the subsequent TTL cycle.

2023-05-30 — {service}. Distributed tracing showed span timestamps from {node} were {clock_offset} out of sequence with all peer nodes, making trace reconstruction unreliable. Concurrently, {pod} was producing elevated JWT validation errors. Both symptoms had the same root cause: chrony on {node} had lost contact with {ntp_server} {jitter} minutes earlier, and drift had accumulated to {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of wall time. A scheduled NTP health check had not been configured for this node.

Incident INC-20240614: {service} distributed lock acquisitions began failing intermittently for operations coordinated through {node}. Lock expiry timestamps computed on {node} — {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of peers — caused locks to appear expired on the coordinator before the holder could renew. Other nodes participated in locking normally. Chrony makestep on {node} resolved the offset; lock failures dropped to zero within one polling interval.

08/19/2023 RCA — {service} — AP-NORTHEAST-1. Between 01:05 and 02:17 UTC, {error_pct}% of authentication requests to {service} returned HTTP 401. All failures were isolated to {pod} on {node}. Tokens with expiry {token_exp} were being validated against {node} local time {current_time}, a skew of {clock_offset} {clock_direction} beyond the {tolerance} tolerance window. Chrony reported stratum {stratum} with last sync {jitter} minutes prior. Manual step correction at 02:17 UTC resolved the incident.

On 2024-01-22, {service} engineering was paged for elevated Raft RPC rejection rates on the production etcd cluster. One follower ({node}) was dropping all inbound heartbeats because peer message timestamps predated its local clock by {clock_offset}. The {node} clock was running {clock_direction} while other nodes were NTP-synchronized. Leader election was unstable for 22 minutes until {node} clock was corrected.

2023-10-11 Post-Mortem — {service}. NTP daemon on {node} crashed at an unknown time. Clock drift accumulated to {clock_offset} {clock_direction} before the monitoring system detected the anomaly via JWT error rate alerts. By the time the alert fired, {pod} had been failing {error_pct}% of authentication requests for approximately 40 minutes. The NTP daemon was restarted and chrony resynced to {ntp_server}; no errors recurred during the subsequent 2-hour observation window.

Tracking ID: CLK-20240509-02. {service} token replay detection misfired on {node}: tokens issued at {token_issued} were flagged as originating from the future by all other nodes, causing them to be rejected as potentially replayed. The root cause was {node} clock running {clock_offset} {clock_direction} of peers — tokens timestamped by {node} appeared ahead of wall time on every other node in the cluster. Clock correction at 11:33 UTC resolved all replay rejections.

On 2023-12-07, {service} engineering identified that chrony on {node} had accumulated {clock_offset} of drift since its last successful poll of {ntp_server} ({jitter} minutes prior). The drift caused JWT tokens — validated against node-local time — to be rejected on {pod} while identical tokens validated successfully on all other nodes. The node-specific failure pattern was the key diagnostic signal. Chrony step correction and NTP daemon restart resolved the incident at 16:48 UTC.

2024-08-14 Incident Report — {service}. Audit log event timestamps from {node} were {clock_offset} out of sequence with the rest of the cluster. The out-of-order timestamps were first noticed during a post-incident trace review. Further investigation revealed the same {node} clock drift was causing JWT validation failures on {pod}: {error_pct}% error rate for traffic routed to that pod versus 0% for all other pods. The dual symptom set — audit log ordering errors and auth failures — both resolved after NTP resync.

Starting at 20:33 UTC on 2023-07-26, {service} users began receiving intermittent 401 errors. The failures were not associated with any deployment event. Error rate analysis showed a clear node-specific pattern: {pod} on {node} was failing {error_pct}% of authentication requests while all other pods succeeded. Chrony on {node} showed jitter of {jitter}ms and offset of {clock_offset} {clock_direction} with last NTP sync to {ntp_server} approximately 55 minutes prior. Manual resync resolved the incident at 21:09 UTC.

Full chronology of clock drift event on 2024-03-25: NTP sync on {node} last succeeded at approximately 08:00 UTC. Drift accumulated to {clock_offset} {clock_direction} by 09:45 UTC. {service} JWT validation errors began at 09:47 UTC when accumulated drift exceeded the {tolerance} tolerance. Monitoring detected the elevated 401 rate at 09:51 UTC. On-call identified the node-specific failure pattern at 10:08 UTC. Chrony step correction applied at 10:14 UTC. Error rate returned to zero within 90 seconds of correction.
